firework_factory.title: Automate Firework Rocket production via Create — build a factory that produces 64+ rockets per minute

subtitle.text: Elytra without rockets is gliding. Elytra with infinite rockets is flight. Automate the rockets. Own the sky.

description: CH.15 · ELYTRA HIGHWAY
▸ The Elytra changes everything about Overworld travel.
The map that took 100+ hours to traverse by foot
takes 10 minutes by air.
But Elytra flight requires Firework Rockets for thrust.
Manual crafting is the bottleneck.
Remove the bottleneck. Automate the rockets.
Then own the entire sky.
◈ FIREWORK ROCKET RECIPE
└ Basic flight rocket: 1 Paper + 1 Gunpowder (no star)
(a firework with no star has no explosion — pure thrust)
└ Duration 3: 3 Gunpowder = longest duration = 3× thrust distance
Tradeoff: costs 3× gunpowder
Recommendation: Duration 1 (1 gunpowder) for fuel efficiency
└ Stack size: 64 — bring multiple stacks for long flights
◈ CREATE FIREWORK FACTORY
└ Paper source: Sugarcane → Paper (Millstone or manual craft)
Sugarcane farm: automate with Create Mechanical Harvester
└ Gunpowder source: Creeper farm → Gunpowder
Creeper farm: trap Creepers, detonate via Redstone automation
OR: Witch farm (slower but passive: Witches drop Gunpowder)
└ Craft automation: Deployer holding Gunpowder + Paper → Rockets
OR: Mechanical Crafter array for bulk crafting
└ Target output: 256 rockets = 4 full stacks = 1 session supply
◈ VIA ROMANA INTEGRATION
└ Via Romana roads give a sprint/walk speed bonus
For ground travel (horses, recruits, carts): use roads
For aerial travel (you + Elytra): fly above roads
└ Road network + Elytra: ground troops on roads,
aerial scouting above — complete kingdom logistics
▸ After the dragon, the world is smaller.
Not because it changed.
Because you changed.
The same mountains that took 3 days to cross before
take 40 seconds at full Elytra speed.
Build the rocket factory and the world
becomes yours in a different way.
▶ Automate Firework Rocket production with Create [check]
▶ Collect 256 Firework Rockets
◇ Rocket acceleration stacking: each rocket during flight
resets your speed to maximum.
Time your rockets at speed decay — not constantly.
Correct timing: roughly every 2-3 seconds.
This gives maximum distance per rocket consumed.
